calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus (Code Of Civil Procedure 1085 And 1094.5)
Off Calendar May-24-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Mark Carton,Esq. 415-567-5517 for Petitioner. (in-person)
Wayne Snodgrass, Deputy city attorney, 415-554-4675 for Respondent.
Stanley Ridell, Esq. 415-787-0576 for Real Party in interest. (in-person)

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court passes the matter to be heard last per the request of the moving party. The matter is recalled at 10:00am. Mr. Carton argues. Mr. Snodgrass and Mr. Ridell responds and agress with the court's tentative ruling. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONERS BRETT MARTY, ANDREW HUTCHINSON'S Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus (Code Of Civil Procedure 1085 and 1094.5).

Taken Under Submission.***

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

***After the hearing, the Court signs an order denying petition for writ of mandate.***

PETITIONER BRETT MARTY, ANDREW HUTCHINSON PETITIONERS NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS (CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 1085 AND 1094.5)
Off Calendar Mar-29-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. MARK CARTON, Esq. (Tel: 415-567-5517) for petitioners;
Atty. STANLEY RIDDELL, Esq. of Law Office of Stanley Riddell (Tel: 415-463-2349) for Real Party in Interest Queen Neal, Trustee of the Bernice Ellis;
Atty. WAYNE K. SNODRGRASS, Esq. of the San Francisco City Attorney's Office (Tel: 415-554-4675) for Respondents City and County of San Francisco, San Francisco Residential Rent Stabilization and Arbitration Board (via Zoom videoconference).

HEARING RE: PETITIONERS BRETT MARTY AND ANDREW HUTCHINSON'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS (CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 1085 AND 1094.5).

Matter discussed.

The Court rules as follows: Petitioners have indicated that they wish to file an amended petition, and the Court will permit the petitioners to do so. Petitioners may set the matter for hearing again after they have filed their amended petition. The Court instructs the parties to deliver a paper hard copy of the Administrative Record to Dept. 302 prior to the hearing. Parties are to meet and confer as to the selection of the hearing date for the matter, and as to the form of the Administrative Record.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Petitioners Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus (Code Of Civil Procedure 1085 And 1094.5)
Off Calendar Mar-15-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: PETITIONER BRETT MARTY, ANDREW HUTCHINSON Petitioners Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus (Code Of Civil Procedure 1085 And 1094.5) is transferred to Department 302 to be heard on March 29, 2023 at 9:30 a.m. per the order of the presiding judge. Respondent must deliver hard copies of their response to department 302 no later than March 17, 2023 by 5:00 p.m.
Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion To Stay Administrative Decision
Off Calendar Dec-29-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER BRETT MARTY, ANDREW HUTCHINSON Notice Of Motion To Stay Administrative Decision

Petitioner's Motion to Stay Administrative Decision is GRANTED. The enforcement is stayed until March 15, 2023. Petitioner to set the writ for hearing for March 15, 2023.

Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: W. Trupek, Not Reported (501/REQ)

Notice Of Motion To Stay Administrative Decision
Off Calendar Dec-22-2022 Continued To Dec-29-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONERS' MOTION TO STAY ADMINISTRATIVE DECISION

Appearances: Mark Carton (415.567.5517) for petitioners and moving parties; Wayne Snodgrass of the City Attorney's Office (415.554.4675) for respondents.

Mr. Carton informs the Court he is unavailable on the continued hearing date of December 30, 2022. The matter is continued to December 29, 2022, by the order of the presiding judge. Plaintiff is to give notice of the hearing date to all parties. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

Notice Of Motion For Order Staying Rent Board Decision
Off Calendar Dec-03-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PETITIONERS BRETT MARTY AND ANDREW HUTCHINSON'S NOTICE OF MOTION FOR ORDER STAYING RENT BOARD DECISION is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Rent Board decision is stayed until February 28, 2021.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
